name: coingecko
description: CoinGecko API documentation - cryptocurrency market data API, price feeds, market cap, volume, historical data. Use when integrating CoinGecko API, building crypto price trackers, or accessing cryptocurrency market data.
capabilities: Retrieve real-time cryptocurrency prices and market data
Get historical price data and market charts
Access market cap, volume, and trading statistics
Query cryptocurrency metadata and contract addresses
Fetch NFT collection data and floor prices
Get exchange listings and trading pair information
Access global DeFi and blockchain statistics
Retrieve coin categories and market indices
Search coins, exchanges, and NFT collections
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: CoinGecko API documentation skill containing cryptocurrency market data references. Static analysis flagged 5874 potential issues across risk categories, but all are FALSE_POSITIVE as this is documentation-only content. Risk factors (external_commands, network, env_access, filesystem) represent mentions in documentation context, not actual executable code. All findings are documentation references to API endpoints, authentication patterns, and cryptocurrency-related terminology that triggers heuristic scanners. Safe for publication with no executable security risks.
valueStatement: Access real-time cryptocurrency prices, market cap, volume, and historical data through the CoinGecko API. Essential for building crypto price trackers, DeFi dashboards, and cryptocurrency trading tools.
userTitle: CoinGecko API Documentation - Cryptocurrency Market Data
limitations: Free API tier has rate limits (10-50 calls/minute)
Historical data granularity limited on free tier
Some advanced endpoints require paid API plan
Cannot execute trades or interact with blockchains directly
Data is sourced from exchanges, may have delays

useCases.title: Crypto Price Tracker
useCases.description: Build a real-time cryptocurrency price tracking dashboard showing current prices, 24h changes, and market cap for top coins.

useCases.title: DeFi Dashboard
useCases.description: Create a DeFi dashboard displaying TVL, yield rates, and protocol statistics from across the ecosystem.

useCases.title: Portfolio Analyzer
useCases.description: Build a portfolio tracking tool that monitors holdings and calculates performance based on historical price data.

useCases.title: Trading Bot Integration
useCases.description: Use CoinGecko API as a price feed source for automated trading strategies and arbitrage bots.

useCases.title: Crypto News Aggregator
useCases.description: Combine market data with trending information to create a crypto news and market sentiment dashboard.

promptTemplates.title: Get Current Price
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the current price and 24h statistics for [COIN_NAME] using the CoinGecko API. Include market cap and trading volume.
promptTemplates.scenario: Looking up current market data for a specific cryptocurrency

promptTemplates.title: Historical Price Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve the last 30 days of price history for [COIN_ID] and format it as a chart data array showing dates and prices.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building historical price charts or analyzing price trends

promptTemplates.title: Top Gainers and Losers
promptTemplates.prompt: List the top 10 cryptocurrencies with the highest 24h percentage gains and the top 10 with the biggest losses.

promptTemplates.title: Exchange Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare trading volumes and fees between [EXCHANGE_1] and [EXCHANGE_2] for the [COIN_PAIR] trading pair.

promptTemplates.title: DeFi Statistics
promptTemplates.prompt: Get current DeFi total value locked (TVL) and list the top 10 DeFi protocols by TVL with their change percentages.
promptTemplates.scenario: DeFi market research and protocol analysis

bestPractices: Cache API responses to respect rate limits
Use the API ID (not symbol) for coin queries to avoid ambiguity
Implement exponential backoff for retry logic
Store CoinGecko API IDs in a mapping for efficient lookups
Check 'is_active' status before relying on exchange data
Use 'vs_currencies' parameter to specify target fiat currency
Implement proper error handling for network failures
Rotate between multiple free API keys if available for higher limits
antiPatterns: Making API calls inside tight loops without caching
Using coin symbols instead of IDs (can be ambiguous)
Ignoring rate limit headers and hitting API blocks
Not handling API downtime or data gaps
Assuming real-time data when using historical endpoints
Storing API keys in client-side code
Querying the same data repeatedly instead of caching

faq.question: Is the CoinGecko API free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, CoinGecko offers a free tier with 10-50 calls per minute. Higher rate limits and advanced endpoints require a paid API plan.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at https://www.coingecko.com/developers/api to get a free API key. Paid plans offer higher limits and additional endpoints.

faq.question: What data can I access without an API key?
faq.answer: Basic endpoints like price, market cap, and simple historical data are available without authentication, but with stricter rate limits.

faq.question: How accurate is the market data?
faq.answer: CoinGecko aggregates data from 800+ exchanges. There may be slight delays compared to individual exchange data. Always verify with primary sources for trading.

faq.question: Can I use this for live trading?
faq.answer: The API is suitable for price feeds and market analysis. For live trading, consider WebSocket feeds from exchanges for lower latency.
